assertions.text: Vehicle was traveling at about 90 km/h prior to the collision.
Vehicle was traveling at highway speed prior to the collision.
Driver applied the brakes only at the last moment before impact.
Vehicle struck the back of a parked vehicle with its front bumper.
There was a single impact in which the front bumper struck the parked vehicle.
The collision involved a front bumper striking a parked vehicle (implying a low-to-moderate-impact contact).
Braking occurred after the approach at highway speed and before the impact (i.e., brakes applied at the last moment, then impact).

verdicts.evidence: The claim states "about [90 km/h](T1)" prior to collision, but the recorded speed samples show a pre-impact speed between [34.0 km/h and 32.0 km/h](T2) with stats giving a first value of [34.0 km/h] and last pre-impact value of [32.0 km/h](T3), so [90 km/h](T1) is contradicted.

The narrative calls it "highway speed", but the vehicle's recorded speed is in the mid-30s km/h: samples span [34.0 km/h] to [32.0 km/h](T2) and the compute_stats summary reports min/max [32.0 km/h]/[36.0 km/h] with first/last [34.0 km/h]/[32.0 km/h](T3), which is inconsistent with typical highway speeds.

The driver said brakes applied at last moment; telemetry shows the brake channel transitions from off to on at [t=-0.5 s](T3) and the brake samples include off until [-0.5 s] then on at [-0.5 s] and [0.0 s](T2), indicating braking began immediately before impact, supporting the claim.

The claim about striking the back of a parked vehicle with the front bumper depends on impact direction and whether the other vehicle was parked; telemetry provides a longitudinal delta-V of [ -18.0 km/h ] at [0.16 s](T3) indicating a forward-facing deceleration consistent with a frontal impact but axis alignment and whether the struck vehicle was parked are not recorded, so the specific statement is unverifiable.

EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded: 1](T1) and crash-pulse delta-V shows a single main longitudinal pulse reaching [ -18.0 km/h ](T3) with no additional pulses, supporting a single impact event.
Severity claim (low-to-moderate) is supported by the recorded longitudinal delta-V magnitude of [ -18.0 km/h ](T3), which is a modest delta-V consistent with a low-to-moderate impact.

Sequence claim: telemetry shows approach speed decreasing slightly from [34.0 km/h] at [ -5.0 s ] to [32.0 km/h] at [0.0 s ](T2) and a brake transition at [ -0.5 s ](T3) followed by impact at t=0 with cumulative delta-V reaching [ -18.0 km/h ] at [0.16 s](T3), supporting that braking occurred after approach and immediately before impact.

result.rationale: Speed-related assertions (a1,a2) are contradicted by recorded pre-impact speeds in the low [30s km/h](T3). Brake timing, single-impact count, delta-V magnitude and sequence are supported by brake transitions and the crash-pulse data ([ -18.0 km/h ] at [0.16 s])(T3) and EDR event count ([1])(T1).

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
